scenes.name: dynamic_joint_constraints
scenes.worldSetup: World::create() fully in code (no files): gravity (0,0,-4), timestep 1e-3, Z-up. Three single-body skeletons each with a FreeJoint + BoxShape (mass 1.0, damping 0.02 on all 6 DOFs, Visual+Collision+Dynamics aspects): 'ball_body' (red, offset from anchor (-3,0,1.8), initial angular vel (0,1.4,0)), 'cylindrical_body' (blue, at (0,0,1.15) with visual offset x+0.35, initial vel angular (0,0,4) + linear (0,0,1.2)), 'weld_body' (green, at (3,0,1.15), initial vel angular (0.7,0,0.9) linear (0.6,0,0.4)). Constraints added once to the ConstraintSolver: BallJointConstraint(body, anchor(-3,0,1.8)), CylindricalJointConstraint(body, axisPoint(0,0,1.15), axis +Z), WeldJointConstraint(body). Scene dressing via SimpleFrames: 3 colored lane pads (boxes), anchor sphere, rail line + ArrowShape axis marker, weld cross lines; plus an osg::Group of osgText screen-aligned labels added to viewer->getRootGroup() (scaled by guiScale). Viewer 960x640 scaled by --gui-scale, camera home eye(5,-7,4) center(0,0,0.9) up +Z, viewer->simulate(true) at start.
scenes.controllers: No WorldNode subclass (plain dart::gui::osg::WorldNode, no customPreStep/PostStep). Logic lives in DynamicJointWidget : ImGuiWidget (anonymous namespace): render() pins a 430x290 (xguiScale) panel to the top-right, menu bar with Menu>Exit (viewer->setDone) and Help>About DART (viewer->showAbout), Play/Pause radio buttons driving viewer->simulate(), read-only Time display, three description lines, and a live constraint count from getConstraintSolver()->getNumConstraints().
keyBindings: (no custom osgGA key handler; built-in ImGuiViewer defaults apply, e.g. Space: simulation on/off)
tunables: Play/Pause radio buttons (ImGui) toggling viewer->simulate()
--gui-scale <value> CLI option scaling ImGui panel, text labels, and window extent (parseGuiScaleOptions/scaleWindowExtent)
scenes.interactions: None: no DnD, no InteractiveFrame, no mouse picking. Only the ImGui panel and default camera manipulator.
scenes.portRisks: osgText label group is added directly to viewer->getRootGroup() and sized from guiScale at construction — in a demos host it must be added/removed per scene and rescaled if gui scale changes. DynamicJointWidget stores an osg::ref_ptr<ImGuiViewer> (viewer<->widget ref cycle pattern). Constraints are registered once at world build; a scene reset/reload must recreate both bodies and constraints (constraint solver state is not otherwise touched). Panel uses ImGuiCond_Always positioning (fights user dragging) and NoSavedSettings. GuiScaleOptions CLI parsing and initial simulate(true) are done in main and need equivalents in the host.

scenes.name: joint_constraints
scenes.worldSetup: SkelParser::readWorld("dart://sample/skel/fullbody1.skel"); gravity explicitly set to (0,-9.81,0) — Y-up world (camera up (0,1,0), home eye (5,3,3)). Biped is world skeleton index 1. Initial pose set via hardcoded generalized-coordinate indices {1,4,6,9,10,13,16,17,21} = (-0.1,0.2,0.2,-0.5,0.3,0.2,-0.5,0.3,-0.1) (global orient/pos y, hips, knees, ankles, lower back). Controller allocated with raw new (deleted after viewer.run()). Window 640x480.
scenes.controllers: JointConstraintsWorld : RealTimeWorldNode. customPreStep: (1) addExtForce(handler force) on BodyNode 'h_spine'; (2) Controller::computeTorques(q, dq) then skeleton->setForces(torques); (3) handler->decrementImpulseDuration() (force auto-clears after 100 steps). customPostStep is a no-op (comment says arrow force viz could go there). Controller = SPD tracking controller: holds initial pose as desired DOFs; Kp/Kd zero on 6 root DOFs, Kp=200/Kd=100 for DOFs 6-21 (lower body+back), Kp=20/Kd=10 above; torques = p+d-Kd*qddot*dt using (M+Kd*dt)^-1 and Coriolis/gravity; plus an ankle strategy in the sagittal plane: when COM-x minus CoP-x (CoP from 'h_heel_left' transform * (0.05,0,0)) < 0.1, adds feedback (k1=20,k2=10,kd=100) to torques[17],[19] (ankles) and [25],[26]; root torques zeroed each step. mConstrForces exists for SPD but is never fed from the solver (stays zero). JointConstraintsEventHandler holds harness state: toggling creates/removes a WeldJointConstraint on BodyNode 'h_pelvis' via the world's ConstraintSolver.
keyBindings: 1: push forward (force +40 N on x of h_spine for 100 steps)
2: push backward (force -40 N x, 100 steps)
3: push right (force +50 N z, 100 steps)
4: push left (force -50 N z, 100 steps)
h: toggle harness (WeldJointConstraint on h_pelvis) on/off
H: same as h
Space: simulation on/off (built-in Viewer default, referenced in instruction text)
tunables: Harness on/off (runtime toggle via key; safe anytime)
Perturbation force magnitudes (compile-time: 40 N x, 50 N z) and impulse duration (100 steps)
SPD gains Kp/Kd and ankle-strategy gains k1=20,k2=10,kd=100 (compile-time; DOF-index coupled)
Desired pose via Controller::setDesiredDof(index, value) (API exists, unused by UI)
scenes.interactions: Keyboard-only via custom osgGA::GUIEventHandler added to the Viewer; no ImGui, no DnD, no picking.
scenes.portRisks: Everything is coupled to fullbody1.skel's exact DOF ordering (hardcoded indices 6..26) and body-node names h_pelvis/h_spine/h_heel_left — any asset change silently breaks the controller. Y-up gravity and Y-up camera conflict with a Z-up demos host default. Harness WeldJointConstraint and impulse countdown live in the event handler; on scene switch the constraint must be removed and handler state reset or the solver keeps a dangling constraint. Controller is a raw pointer owned by main. computeTorques inverts the full mass matrix every step (O(n^3), fine for this model but per-step cost in host). No custom instruction for '1'-'4' beyond stdout prints.

scenes.name: human_joint_limits
scenes.worldSetup: SkelParser::readWorld(DART_DATA_PATH "/skel/kima/kima_human_edited.skel") — compile-time data path, not a dart:// URI. Skeleton named 'human'; setLimitEnforcement(true) on every joint. No explicit gravity/timestep changes (skel file defaults). Four custom neural-net joint-limit constraints are created lazily on the FIRST customPreRefresh (not in main): HumanArmJointLimitConstraint(j_bicep_left, j_forearm_left, mirror=false) and (j_bicep_right, j_forearm_right, mirror=true); HumanLegJointLimitConstraint(j_thigh_left, j_shin_left, j_heel_left, false) and (j_thigh_right, j_shin_right, j_heel_right, true); each added to the world's ConstraintSolver. Window 640x480, plain dart::gui::osg::Viewer.
scenes.controllers: HumanJointLimitsWorldNode : WorldNode (not RealTimeWorldNode). customPreRefresh: one-shot constraint initialization guarded by mConstraintsInitialized; contains a commented-out external-force snippet on 'l-lowerarm'. The constraints themselves are full ConstraintBase implementations: update() evaluates a tiny_dnn network C(q) on trig features of shoulder(3)+elbow(1) [arm] or hip(3)+knee(1)+ankle(2) [leg] DOFs (with sign-mirroring of qz,qy for right limbs), activates when C-0.5<=0, computes the constraint Jacobian by manual layer-by-layer backprop through the net, then getInformation() builds a 1-D unilateral LCP row (lo=0, hi=dInfinity, ERP-style bouncing velocity, warm-start via mOldX/mLifeTime); applyUnitImpulse/getVelocityChange/applyImpulse map the Jacobian onto joint constraint impulses; getRootSkeleton() reads skeleton->mUnionRootSkeleton.
keyBindings: (HumanJointLimitsEventHandler is a stub that returns false for all keys)
Space: simulation on/off (built-in Viewer default, printed to stdout)
tunables: HumanArmJointLimitConstraint / HumanLegJointLimitConstraint static setters (per-class globals): setErrorAllowance (default 0.0, clamped >=0), setErrorReductionParameter (default 0.01, clamped [0,1]), setMaxErrorReductionVelocity (default 1e+1, clamped >=0), setConstraintForceMixing (default 1e-9, clamped [1e-9,1])
(no ImGui panel; no runtime UI — all tunables are API-level)
scenes.interactions: None: no picking, no DnD; keyboard handler is a no-op placeholder.
assets: DART_DATA_PATH /humanJointLimits/neuralnets/net-larm (tiny_dnn weights, loaded in arm-constraint ctor)
DART_DATA_PATH /humanJointLimits/neuralnets/net-lleg (tiny_dnn weights, loaded in leg-constraint ctor)
scenes.portRisks: Hard optional dependency on tiny_dnn (CMake silently disables the example if TinyDNN or Threads is missing — the demos host needs the same conditional compile) plus links dart-collision-bullet, dart-collision-ode, dart-utils-urdf even though main.cpp only includes their headers. Constraint .cpps include the internal header dart/external/odelcpsolver/lcp.h (for dInfinity) and touch Skeleton::mUnionRootSkeleton — both tie the code to DART internals and to the LCP (boxed-LCP) solver path; will not work under other constraint solvers. Static per-class tunables are process-global: two scenes or repeated loads share/overwrite them. Lazy constraint creation in customPreRefresh must be re-armed and old constraints removed from the solver on scene reload. DART_DATA_PATH is baked at compile time (breaks relocatable installs; prefer dart:// retriever in the port). tiny_dnn header is heavy and can conflict with parallel-for/threading settings of the host build.

scenes.name: mixed_chain
scenes.worldSetup: SkelParser::readWorld("dart://sample/skel/test/test_articulated_bodies_10bodies.skel") — a hybrid chain containing soft bodies. No gravity/timestep overrides (from skel file). Initial pose: skeleton index 1's first 3 DOFs randomized uniform(-0.5,0.5) via dart::math::Random. Camera home eye(2,1,2) up (0,0,1); window 640x480; controls printed to stdout.
scenes.controllers: MixedChainWorldNode : RealTimeWorldNode. customPreStep calls MixedChainEventHandler::applyForces(): while mImpulseDuration>0 it calls skeleton(1)->getSoftBodyNode(3)->addExtForce(mForceOnRigidBody) (guarded by skeleton && getNumSoftBodyNodes()>3), decrements the counter, and zeroes the force when it hits 0. Handler stores the pending force vector and a 100-step duration set on keypress.
keyBindings: q: apply -500 N in X to soft body node 3 for 100 steps
w: apply +500 N in X (100 steps)
e: apply -500 N in Y (100 steps)
r: apply +500 N in Y (100 steps)
t: apply -500 N in Z (100 steps)
y: apply +500 N in Z (100 steps)
Space: simulation on/off (built-in Viewer default, printed in stdout help)
tunables: FORCE_ON_RIGIDBODY = 500.0 N (compile-time macro; despite the name, applied to a SoftBodyNode)
Impulse duration = 100 steps (compile-time)
Random initial-pose range uniform(-0.5,0.5) on first 3 DOFs (compile-time)
scenes.interactions: Keyboard-only custom GUIEventHandler; no ImGui, no picking, no DnD.
scenes.portRisks: Keys q/w/e/r/t/y are prime candidates to collide with a consolidated host's own bindings (e.g. 'r' also means 'restart' in soft_bodies). Force state lives in the event handler and is consumed by the WorldNode — on scene switch both must be reset together or a stale impulse fires into the new scene. Hardcoded assumptions: world skeleton index 1, soft body node index 3, >3 soft body nodes. Uses global dart::math::Random (seeding affects reproducibility across demos). Soft-body rendering requires SoftMeshShape support in the host's OSG shape frontend.

scenes.name: soft_bodies
scenes.worldSetup: SkelParser::readWorld("dart://sample/skel/softBodies.skel"). No gravity/timestep overrides. RecordingWorld node created around the world and node->simulate(true) is called before viewer attach. Window 640x480 scaled by --gui-scale (parseGuiScaleOptions). No custom camera home.
scenes.controllers: RecordingWorld : RealTimeWorldNode implements a state-history scrubber: grabTimeSlice() snapshots every skeleton's Skeleton::Configuration plus every BodyNode's composite Aspect state (captures soft-body point states) into mHistory; ctor grabs frame 0. customPostStep: if the cursor is behind the end (user rewound then resumed), truncates the future (mHistory.resize(mCurrentIndex+1)) before appending the new slice and advancing mCurrentIndex — branching timeline semantics. moveTo(index): pauses simulation via mViewer->simulate(false), clamps index, restores setConfiguration + setCompositeState for all skeletons/bodies, prints the frame number. Helpers moveForward/moveBackward(delta), restart()=moveTo(0), moveToEnd(). RecordingEventHandler maps keys to these.
keyBindings: [: step playback backward 1 recorded frame (pauses sim)
]: step playback forward 1 frame (pauses sim)
{: step backward 10 frames
}: step forward 10 frames
r: restart — jump to recorded frame 0
\: jump to the latest recorded frame
Space: simulation on/off (built-in Viewer default; resuming from mid-history truncates the 'future' frames)
tunables: --gui-scale <value> CLI option (window extent scaling only; no ImGui panel)
Playback cursor position (via keys; effectively the only runtime control)
scenes.interactions: Keyboard-only custom GUIEventHandler; no ImGui, no picking, no DnD.
scenes.portRisks: Unbounded mHistory growth (full world state every step at real-time rate — memory grows fast; a host should cap or ring-buffer it). moveTo() dereferences the WorldNode's mViewer (set only after addWorldNode) — calling before attach crashes; in a host, guard it. History restore assumes the skeleton set and per-skeleton body counts never change during the run. 'r' and bracket keys may clash with other demos' bindings in a consolidated app. History must be cleared on scene switch or it pins the old world's state objects. Soft-body rendering needs SoftMeshShape support; RealTimeWorldNode pacing means recorded frames are timestep-spaced, not render-spaced.

scenes.name: tinkertoy
scenes.worldSetup: Empty dart::simulation::World built in code (default gravity (0,0,-9.81), Z-up). A disabled-tool InteractiveFrame 'coordinates' (scale 0.2) is added as a world-origin axis reference. TinkertoyWorldNode ctor: creates a shared InteractiveFrame drag target added as a SimpleFrame; creates reusable shapes flagged DYNAMIC_COLOR (weld box 2r x w x w, revolute cylinder r x 1.5w, ball sphere r, block box 0.5x0.075x0.075 with +x/2 offset); builds two initial toys entirely in code from Ball/Revolute/Weld-joint blocks at various rotated transforms (block mass from balsa density 0.16e4 kg/m3, per-DOF damping 0.4, inertia set from block shape with COM at half length); creates a DYNAMIC_VERTICES LineSegmentShape 'force line' (orange). New bodies are added to the collision group explicitly; RevoluteJoint axis set to +Z of the target. Window 1280x720 scaled by --gui-scale; window renamed 'Tinkertoy' after realize().
scenes.controllers: TinkertoyWorldNode : RealTimeWorldNode. customPreRefresh: recolors all skeletons (blue DefaultSimulationColor while simulating, yellow paused), overlays picked node color (fuchsia while simulating, red when paused), and updates the force-line vertices from picked point to target. customPreStep: if a node is picked, applies spring force F = mForceCoeff * (target_pos - picked_point_world) clamped to MaxForce=200 N at the picked body-local point via addExtForce. setupViewer() enables DnD on the target InteractiveFrame. addBlock<JointType>() refuses while simulating (prints 'pause first'), creates a new skeleton when nothing is picked, wires shapes/inertia/collision registration, and clears the pick. deletePick() (paused only) removes the picked subtree, unregisters its DnD and collision frames. reorientTarget() zeroes the target rotation. Force coeff clamped to [10,1000], +/-10 per keypress. TinkertoyWidget : ImGuiWidget renders the control panel (see tunables). TinkertoyMouseHandler : MouseEventHandler converts LEFT_MOUSE BUTTON_PUSH picks into handlePick() (stores body + local point, moves target to pick position offset along the normal).
keyBindings: Tab: reset camera to home position (viewer->home())
1: attach new block to selection with WeldJoint (paused only)
2: attach new block with RevoluteJoint (axis = target z) (paused only)
3: attach new block with BallJoint (paused only)
Backspace: clear selection / reset target
Delete: delete picked block and all its children (paused only)
Up: increase force coefficient by 10 (max 1000)
Down: decrease force coefficient by 10 (min 10)
`: reorient drag target to identity rotation
Return/Enter: start recording screenshots to DART_DATA_PATH /screencap, or pause recording if active
Space: play/pause simulation (built-in ImGuiViewer default; referenced throughout help text)
NOTE: ImGui help text advertises '[G] to toggle Gravity' but NO key handler for G exists — gravity is only toggleable via the ImGui checkbox (parity bug to preserve or fix consciously)
tunables: Force Coeff slider (ImGui SliderFloat, 10..1000, directly bound to mNode->mForceCoeff; also Up/Down keys in same range)
Gravity On/Off checkbox (toggles world gravity between -9.81 Z and zero)
Headlights On/Off checkbox (viewer->switchHeadlights)
Play/Pause radio buttons (viewer->simulate)
Buttons: Reorient Target, Reset Target (clearPick), Add a Weld-Joint Block, Add a Revolute-Joint Block, Add a Ball-Joint Block, Delete Block
--gui-scale <value> CLI option (panel + window scaling)
scenes.interactions: Richest of the set: left-mouse picking of blocks via dart::gui::osg::MouseEventHandler + getDefaultEventHandler()->getButtonPicks(LEFT_MOUSE, BUTTON_PUSH); drag-and-drop of the target InteractiveFrame (enabled in setupViewer, also enable/disable dance during deletePick); force line visual tracks pick->target; per-frame recoloring communicates sim/paused/selected states.
assets: DART_DATA_PATH /screencap (output directory for Enter-key screen recording)
scenes.portRisks: mPickedNode and mPickedPoint are NOT initialized in the TinkertoyWorldNode constructor (only mForceCoeff/mWasSimulating are) — customPreRefresh reads mPickedNode before any pick, i.e. latent UB that happens to be benign today; the port should zero-init. Help text/keybinding mismatch on [G]. setupViewer() ordering: DnD on the target only works after the node is attached to a viewer; the widget also holds ref_ptr<ImGuiViewer>. deletePick and addBlock mutate the collision group and DnD registry — a consolidated host must tear these down on scene exit. Enter-key recording writes into the source data path (bad for installed/consolidated apps). Window rename via realize()+getWindows() is host-specific. mForceCoeff is float but APIs use double; SliderFloat binds the raw member, bypassing setForceCoeff clamping (slider range enforces it anyway).

scenes.name: heightmap
scenes.worldSetup: World::create() with gravity explicitly ZERO; no skeletons. A SimpleFrame holds a procedurally generated HeightmapShape<float> (template S=float): 100x100 resolution, 2x2 m, heights random-uniform in [0, 0.1] via dart::math::Random, scale set to (xSize/xRes, ySize/yRes, 1), frame translated to (-xSize/2, +ySize/2) so the map is centered. HeightmapWorld : WorldNode with empty customPreStep; node->setNumStepsPerCycle(16). GridVisual attachment added via viewer.addAttachment(grid). ImGuiViewer, simulate(true) at start, window 1280x720 scaled by --gui-scale, camera home eye(2.57,3.14,1.64) center(0,0,0.3) up(-0.24,-0.25,0.94).
scenes.controllers: No physics logic (customPreStep empty; world contains only a SimpleFrame, so stepping does nothing observable). HeightmapWidget<float> : ImGuiWidget owns the terrain parameters (xRes,yRes,xSize,ySize,zMin,zMax) and on any change calls updateHeightmapShape(), which REPLACES the frame's shape with a freshly generated random heightmap and re-centers the frame. Panel (titled, stale, 'Point Cloud & Voxel Grid Demo') has menu Exit/About, Help section echoing viewer instructions, Play/Pause radios, the Heightmap section, and a full GridVisual editor.
keyBindings: (no custom key handler; built-in ImGuiViewer defaults, e.g. Space: play/pause)
tunables: Terrain Show checkbox (VisualAspect show/hide)
X Resolution / Y Resolution (InputInt, step 5/10, clamped >= 5; regenerates heightmap)
X Size / Y Size (InputFloat, step 0.1/0.2, clamped >= 0.1; regenerates)
Z Min / Z Max (InputFloat, step 0.05/0.1; regenerates; NOT validated zMin<=zMax)
Terrain Color (ColorEdit4 -> VisualAspect RGBA)
Grid Show checkbox (GridVisual::display)
Grid plane radios XY / YZ / ZX (setPlaneType)
Grid offset X/Y/Z (InputFloat, 3 columns)
Grid Line Count (InputInt, clamped >= 0, setNumCells)
Grid Line Step Size (InputFloat, setMinorLineStepSize)
Minor Lines per Major Line (InputInt, clamped >= 0)
Axis Line Width / Major Line Width / Minor Line Width (InputFloat)
Major Line Color / Minor Line Color (ColorEdit3)
Play/Pause radio buttons
--gui-scale <value> CLI option
scenes.interactions: None beyond the ImGui panel and default camera manipulator; no picking, no DnD.
scenes.portRisks: render() uses several function-local statistics (grid offset, cellCount, cellStepSize, minorLinesPerMajorLine, axisLineWidth, majorLineWidth, majorColor) — they persist across widget instances/scene switches in a consolidated app and would leak state between demos; convert to members in the port. Panel title is stale ('Point Cloud & Voxel Grid Demo') and description says 'TODO.'. Every parameter tweak allocates a brand-new random HeightmapShape (fine visually; if the host adds collision against terrain, regeneration invalidates collision objects). GridVisual lifetime is a viewer attachment (viewer.addAttachment) — the host must detach on scene exit. Uses global dart::math::Random. zMin/zMax unvalidated relative ordering. Widget is templated (HeightmapWidget<float>) and grid radios are oddly gated behind isAllowingSimulation().
